name: GitHub Packages
cost: Free for public packages and the Container registry. Private packages billed on storage + egress against per-plan quotas (Free 500 MB storage / 1 GB transfer; Pro & Team 2 GB / 10 GB; Enterprise Cloud 50 GB + 100 GB transfer included); overage $0.25/GB-month storage and $0.50/GB data-transfer-out (free inside Actions). Bundled with GitHub plans: Free, Team ~$4/user/mo, Enterprise Cloud ~$21/user/mo. None of this applies to Python, which is not a supported ecosystem.
language: Proprietary, mixed (Ruby on Rails + Go); not open source
last_updated: 2026 (continuously updated managed service; PyPI support was formally cancelled 2023-06-07)
repo: No public source repo (closed source). Public issue/roadmap tracker: https://github.com/github/roadmap
perf_notes: Managed, CDN-fronted SaaS; GitHub publishes no throughput/latency/memory benchmarks for Packages. Self-hosted GitHub Enterprise Server performance depends on instance sizing and the external object store (S3/Azure/MinIO). Moot for Python, which cannot be hosted or served.
scale_notes: Operates at GitHub.com global scale as a fully managed service; self-hosted GitHub Enterprise Server supports HA/clustering and scale-out backed by external blob storage. None of this benefits Python because the PyPI ecosystem is unsupported.
summary: GitHub's repository-integrated package registry for npm, RubyGems, Maven/Gradle, NuGet and Docker/OCI; it has no native Python/PyPI support (the PyPI roadmap item was cancelled "not planned" in 2023), so it cannot host, proxy, or serve Python packages to pip/uv/twine.
extras: NO native Python/PyPI support: official docs list only npm, RubyGems, Maven, Gradle, NuGet and Docker/OCI; roadmap issue github/roadmap#94 'Packages: Python (PyPi) support' was closed as not_planned on 2023-06-07 (verified via GitHub API: state_reason=not_planned, closed_at 2023-06-07T15:41:07Z)

Common Python 'workarounds' (pip install git+https://..., or a static PEP 503 index served from GitHub Pages/Releases) are NOT GitHub Packages and are unauthenticated package-manager features, not a registry
GitHub Container Registry (ghcr.io) for Docker/OCI artifacts with cosign/Sigstore signing
npm package provenance: keyless Sigstore (Fulcio/Rekor) signing via Actions OIDC, logged to a public transparency ledger (npm/container ecosystems only, not Python)
GitHub Artifact Attestations: SLSA build provenance and signed SBOMs for any Actions-built artifact
Deep GitHub Actions integration: GITHUB_TOKEN auto-authenticates publish/install in workflows
Package permissions inherited from the linked repository, with optional granular per-user/per-org access; some registries are repo-scoped only
Self-hosted via GitHub Enterprise Server, which requires external blob storage (AWS S3 / S3-compatible MinIO / Azure Blob)
Org/enterprise audit log records package publish/delete/access events and can be streamed (JSON)
Dependabot / GitHub Advisory Database scans Python (pip/Poetry) dependency manifests in repos for CVEs, but this is repo dependency scanning, not scanning of packages hosted in the registry
No download/usage statistics surfaced for hosted packages (long-standing community gap)
